client (for B2 native API key management)\n\t// Uses the same B2 credentials as the S3 backend\n\tvar b2keysClient *b2keys.Client\n\tif cfg.Bucket != \"\" {\n\t\t// Bucket-scoped configuration: application key is restricted to a single bucket\n\t\t// and cannot call account-level B2 API endpoints (like b2_authorize_account).\n\t\t// Key management is unavailable in this mode, which is expected and intentional.\n\t\tlogger.Info(\"B2 key management disabled (application key is bucket-scoped)\")\n\t} else {\n\t\t// No bucket restriction: attempt to create B2 keys client for account-level operations\n\t\t// Extract account ID from the key ID (B2 key IDs are in format accountIdKeyId)\n\t\t// The account ID is the first 12 characters\n\t\taccountID := cfg.B2AccessKeyID\n\t\tif len(accountID) > 12 {\n\t\t\taccountID = accountID[:12]\n\t\t}\n\t\tb2keysClient, err = b2keys.NewClient(context.Background(), accountID, cfg.B2SecretAccessKey)\n\t\tif err blobs via Cloudflare for free egress and edge\n\t\t// caching. Fall back to direct B2 if no Cloudflare domain is configured.\n\t\tfetcher := func(ctx context.Context, key string) ([]byte, error) {\n\t\t\tvar body io.ReadCloser\n\t\t\tvar ferr error\n\t\t\tif cfg.CFDomain != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tbody, _, ferr = b2Backend.Get(ctx, cfg.Bucket, key)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tbody, _, ferr = b2Backend.GetDirect(ctx, cfg.Bucket, key)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif ferr != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, ferr\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdefer body.Close()\n\t\t\treturn io.ReadAll(body)\n\t\t}\n\t\tloadStart := time.Now()\n\t\tif loadErr := manifest.Load(context.Background(), manifestIdx, cfg.ManifestPrefix, cfg.WriterID, lister, fetcher); loadErr != nil {\n\t\t\tlogger.WithFields(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\t\"error\": loadErr.Error(),\n\t\t\t}).Warn(\"manifest startup load failed — continuing with empty manifest index\")\n\t\t\tmanifestIdx = manifest.New() // reset to empty on error\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tlogger.WithFields(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\t\"entries\":  manifestIdx.Len(),\n\t\t\t\t\"seq\":      manifestIdx.Seq(),\n\t\t\t\t\"duration\": time.Since(loadStart).String(),\n\t\t\t}).Info(\"manifest index loaded\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Create and start the manifest writer (async delta persistence to B2).\n\t// Uploads go direct to B2 (free ingress); no Cloudflare path needed here.\n\tvar manifestWriter *manifest.Writer\n\tvar manifestCompactor *manifest.Compactor\n\tif manifestIdx != nil {\n\t\tuploader := func(ctx context.Context, key string, data []byte) error {\n\t\t\treturn b2Backend.Put(ctx, cfg.Bucket, key, bytes.NewReader(data), int64(len(data)), nil)\n\t\t}\n\t\tmanifestWriter = manifest.NewWriter(manifestIdx, cfg.ManifestPrefix, cfg.WriterID, uploader, 0)\n\n\t\t// Create compactor that lists and batch-deletes via the B2 backend.\n\t\tlisterForCompactor := func(ctx context.Context, prefix, token string) ([]string, string, error) {\n\t\t\tresult, lerr := b2Backend.ListRaw(ctx, cfg.Bucket, prefix, \"\", token, 1000)\n\t\t\tif lerr != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, \"\", lerr\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tkeys := make([]string, len(result.Objects))\n\t\t\tfor i, obj := range result.Objects {\n\t\t\t\tkeys[i] = obj.Key\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn keys, result.NextToken, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tdeleter := func(ctx context.Context, keys []string) error {\n\t\t\treturn b2Backend.DeleteObjects(ctx, cfg.Bucket, keys)\n\t\t}\n\t\tcompactionInterval := time.Duration(cfg.ManifestCompactionInterval) * time.Second\n\t\tmanifestCompactor = manifest.NewCompactor(\n\t\t\tmanifestIdx,\n\t\t\tcfg.ManifestPrefix,\n\t\t\tcfg.WriterID,\n\t\t\tuploader,\n\t\t\tlisterForCompactor,\n\t\t\tdeleter,\n\t\t\tcompactionInterval,\n\t\t\tcfg.ManifestCompactionThreshold,\n\t\t)\n\n\t\t// Wire writer → compactor: after each delta flush, notify compactor.\n\t\tmanifestWriter.SetOnFlush(manifestCompactor.NotifyDelta)\n\n\t\tmanifestWriter.Start(context.Background())\n\t\tmanifestCompactor.Start(context.Background())\n\t\tlogger.Info(\"manifest writer and compactor started\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn &Server{\n\t\tconfig:         cfg,\n\t\tbackend:        b2Backend,\n\t\tcache:          cache,\n\t\tfooterCache:    footerCache,\n\t\tlistCache:      listCache,\n\t\tkeyManager:     keyMgr,\n\t\tcanary:         canaryMonitor,\n\t\tcanaryDisabled: cfg.CanaryDisabled,\n\t\tprovenance:     provenanceMgr,\n\t\tpresigner:      presigner,\n\t\tb2keys:         b2keysClient,\n\t\tdashboard:      dash,\n\t\tmanifest:          manifestIdx,\n\t\tmanifestWriter:    manifestWriter,\n\t\tmanifestCompactor: manifestCompactor,\n\t\tmetrics:        metrics.DefaultMetrics,\n\t\trequestTracker: metrics.DefaultRequestTracker,\n\t\tlogger:         logger,\n\t}, nil\n}\n\n// manifestRecorder implements handlers.ManifestRecorder. It updates the\n// in-memory index synchronously (so subsequent HeadObject calls see the new\n// state immediately) and enqueues the op for async B2 delta persistence.\ntype manifestRecorder struct {\n\tidx    *manifest.Index\n\twriter *manifest.Writer\n}\n\nfunc (m *manifestRecorder) RecordPut(bucket, key string, size int64, sha256Hex string, iv, wrappedDEK []byte, blockSize int, contentType, etag string) {\n\tentry := &manifest.Entry{\n\t\tPlaintextSize:   size,\n\t\tPlaintextSHA256: sha256Hex,\n\t\tIV:              iv,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:      wrappedDEK,\n\t\tBlockSize:       blockSize,\n\t\tContentType:     contentType,\n\t\tETag:            etag,\n\t\tLastModified:    time.Now().UTC(),\n\t}\n\tm.idx.Put(bucket, key, entry)\n\tm.writer.EnqueuePut(bucket, key, entry)\n}\n\nfunc (m *manifestRecorder) RecordDelete(bucket, key string) {\n\tm.idx.Delete(bucket, key)\n\tm.writer.EnqueueDelete(bucket, key)\n}\n\nfunc (m *manifestRecorder) Lookup(bucket, key string) (*handlers.ManifestEntry, bool) {\n\tentry, ok := m.idx.Get(bucket, key)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, false\n\t}\n\treturn &handlers.ManifestEntry{\n\t\tPlaintextSize: entry.PlaintextSize,\n\t\tContentType:   entry.ContentType,\n\t\tETag:          entry.ETag,\n\t\tLastModified:  entry.LastModified,\n\t\tIV:            entry.IV,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:    entry.WrappedDEK,\n\t\tBlockSize:     entry.BlockSize,\n\t}, true\n}\n\n// StopManifestWriter flushes any pending manifest ops and stops the async writer.\nfunc (s *Server) StopManifestWriter() {\n\tif s.manifestWriter != nil {\n\t\ts.manifestWriter.Stop()\n\t\ts.logger.Info(\"manifest writer stopped\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// StopManifestCompactor stops the background compaction goroutine.\nfunc (s *Server) StopManifestCompactor() {\n\tif s.manifestCompactor != nil {\n\t\ts.manifestCompactor.Stop()\n\t\ts.logger.Info(\"manifest compactor stopped\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// StartCanary starts the canary monitor.\n// It should be called after the server is created.\n// When ARMOR_CANARY_DISABLED=true, the monitor is not started and /readyz\n// always returns 200 regardless of B2 reachability.\nfunc (s *Server) StartCanary(ctx context.Context) {\n\tif s.canary == nil || s.canaryStarted {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif s.canaryDisabled {\n\t\ts.logger.Info(\"Canary monitor disabled (ARMOR_CANARY_DISABLED=true)\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\ts.canaryStarted = true\n\ts.canary.Start(ctx)\n\ts.logger.Info(\"Canary monitor started\")\n}\n\n// StopCanary stops the canary monitor.\nfunc (s *Server) StopCanary() {\n\tif s.canary != nil && s.canaryStarted {\n\t\ts.canary.Stop()\n\t\ts.logger.Info(\"Canary monitor stopped\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// Handler returns the main S3 API handler.\nfunc (s *Server) Handler() http.Handler {\n\tmux := http.NewServeMux()\n\n\t// Health endpoints\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/healthz\", s.healthz)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/readyz\", s.readyz)\n\n\t// Share endpoint for pre-signed URLs (public, no auth required)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/share/\", s.handleShare)\n\n\t// S3 operations\n\th := handlers.New(s.config, s.backend, s.cache, s.footerCache, s.keyManager, s.listCache)\n\n\t// Wire up provenance if available\n\tif s.provenance != nil {\n\t\th.WithProvenance(s.provenance)\n\t}\n\n\t// Wire up manifest recorder if enabled\n\tif s.manifest != nil && s.manifestWriter != nil {\n\t\th.WithManifest(&manifestRecorder{idx: s.manifest, writer: s.manifestWriter})\n\t}\n\n\t// Bucket operations\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/\", s.wrapHandler(h.HandleRoot))\n\n\treturn mux\n}\n\n// AdminHandler returns the admin API handler.\nfunc (s *Server) AdminHandler() http.Handler {\n\tmux := http.NewServeMux()\n\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/healthz\", s.healthz)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/key/verify\", s.verifyKey)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/key/rotate\", s.rotateKey)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/key/export\", s.exportKey)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/armor/canary\", s.canaryHandler)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/armor/audit\", s.audit)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/presign\", s.handlePresign)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/b2/keys\", s.handleB2Keys)           // GET=List, POST=Create\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/b2/keys/\", s.handleB2KeyDelete)     // DELETE=Delete by ID\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/metrics\", s.metrics.Handler())\n\n\t// Dashboard routes (authenticated via DashboardUser/Pass or DashboardToken)\n\tif s.dashboard != nil {\n\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard\", s.dashboard.HandlerWithAuth())\n\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/\", s.dashboard.HandlerWithAuth()) // For prefix navigation\n\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/object\", s.dashboard.ObjectDetailHandlerWithAuth())\n\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/metrics\", s.dashboard.MetricsHandlerWithAuth())\n\n\t\t// Key rotation proxy handler (authenticated)\n\t\tadminClient := &http.Client{\n\t\t\tTimeout: 30 * time.Minute, // Key rotation can take a long time\n\t\t}\n\t\tadminURL := \"http://\" + s.config.AdminListen + \"/admin/key/rotate\"\n\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/admin/key/rotate\", s.dashboard.KeyRotateHandlerWithAuth(adminClient, adminURL))\n\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/admin/key/status\", s.dashboard.KeyRotateStatusHandlerWithAuth())\n\t}\n\n\treturn mux\n}\n\n// healthz returns the health status.\n// This is the liveness probe — it only checks that the process is alive.\n// Backend connectivity is checked by /readyz (the readiness probe).\nfunc (s *Server) healthz(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tw.Write([]byte(\"OK\"))\n}\n\n// readyz returns the readiness status.\n// When the canary monitor is running and not disabled, its in-memory health\n// state is the sole signal — no backend call is made. When the canary is\n// disabled (ARMOR_CANARY_DISABLED=true), /readyz always returns 200 and the\n// liveness probe (/healthz) is the sole health guard. When the canary is not\n// configured, the manifest writer's last flush is used as the health signal.\nfunc (s *Server) readyz(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t// When canary is explicitly disabled, skip all backend checks.\n\tif s.canaryDisabled {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"Ready\"))\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Canary monitor is authoritative when running.\n\tif s.canary != nil && s.canaryStarted {\n\t\tif !s.canary.IsHealthy() {\n\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusServiceUnavailable)\n\t\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"Not ready - canary check failed\"))\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"Ready\"))\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Fallback: manifest writer's last successful delta flush.\n\t// A flush within the last 60 seconds indicates the service is healthy.\n\tconst flushThreshold = 60 * time.Second\n\tif s.manifestWriter != nil {\n\t\tlastFlush := s.manifestWriter.LastFlush()\n\t\tif !lastFlush.IsZero() && time.Since(lastFlush) < flushThreshold {\n\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"Ready\"))\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\t// No recent flush — report unhealthy\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusServiceUnavailable)\n\t\tif lastFlush.IsZero() {\n\t\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"Not ready - manifest writer has never flushed\"))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tw.Write([]byte(fmt.Sprintf(\"Not ready - manifest writer last flush %v ago (threshold %v)\", time.Since(lastFlush).Round(time.Second), flushThreshold)))\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Neither canary nor manifest writer available — report unhealthy.\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusServiceUnavailable)\n\tw.Write([]byte(\"Not ready - no health signal available\"))\n}\n\n// verifyKey verifies the MEK is correct.\nfunc (s *Server) verifyKey(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif r.Method != http.MethodGet {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif s.canary == nil {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tw.Write([]byte(`{\"status\":\"unknown\",\"error\":\"canary monitor not configured\"}`))\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tstatus := s.canary.GetStatus()\n\tif status.DecryptVerified && status.HMACVerified {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tw.Write([]byte(`{\"status\":\"verified\",\"message\":\"MEK is correct\"}`))\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusServiceUnavailable)\n\tw.Write([]byte(`{\"status\":\"unverified\",\"error\":\"canary check failed - MEK may be incorrect\"}`))\n}\n\n// rotateKey rotates the MEK.\nfunc (s *Server) rotateKey(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif r.Method != http.MethodPost {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Read the new MEK from the request body\n\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read request body: %v\", err), http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tdefer r.Body.Close()\n\n\t// The new MEK should be a 32-byte hex-encoded string (64 hex chars)\n\t// or raw 32 bytes\n\tvar newMEK []byte\n\tif len(body) == 64 {\n\t\t// Hex-encoded\n\t\tnewMEK, err = hex.DecodeString(string(body))\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Invalid hex-encoded MEK\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t} else if len(body) == 32 {\n\t\t// Raw bytes\n\t\tnewMEK = body\n\t} else {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Invalid MEK length: expected 32 bytes or 64 hex chars, got %d\", len(body)), http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Create key rotator with the default key. Pass the manifest index so\n\t// rotateObject can skip per-object HeadObject calls when the entry is cached.\n\tdefaultKey := s.keyManager.DefaultKey()\n\trotator := NewKeyRotator(s.backend, s.config.Bucket, defaultKey.MEK, newMEK, s.manifest)\n\n\t// Perform rotation\n\tresult, err := rotator.Rotate(r.Context())\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"status\":  \"failed\",\n\t\t\t\"error\":   err.Error(),\n\t\t\t\"result\":  result,\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Update the server's MEK on success\n\tif result.Status == \"completed\" {\n\t\tif err := s.keyManager.UpdateDefaultKey(newMEK); err != nil {\n\t\t\ts.logger.WithFields(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\t\"error\": err.Error(),\n\t\t\t}).Error(\"failed to update key manager after rotation\")\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Clear the metadata cache since DEKs are now wrapped with new MEK\n\t\ts.cache.Clear()\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(result)\n}\n\n// exportKey exports the current MEK.\nfunc (s *Server) exportKey(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif r.Method != http.MethodGet {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif r.URL.Query().Get(\"confirm\") != \"yes\" {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Must include ?confirm=yes to export key\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Export the default MEK as hex-encoded string\n\tdefaultKey := s.keyManager.DefaultKey()\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]string{\n\t\t\"mek\":   hex.EncodeToString(defaultKey.MEK),\n\t\t\"format\": \"hex\",\n\t\t\"warning\": \"This key provides access to all encrypted data. Store securely.\",\n\t})\n}\n\n// canaryHandler returns the canary status.\nfunc (s *Server) canaryHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif r.Method != http.MethodGet {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif s.canary == nil {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tw.Write([]byte(`{\"status\":\"unknown\",\"error\":\"canary monitor not configured\"}`))\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tstatus := s.canary.GetStatus()\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(status)\n}\n\n// audit returns the audit status.\nfunc (s *Server) audit(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif r.Method != http.MethodGet {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Create auditor and perform audit\n\tauditor := provenance.NewAuditor(s.backend, s.config.Bucket)\n\tresult, err := auditor.Audit(r.Context())\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"status\": \"error\",\n\t\t\t\"error\":   err.Error(),\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(result)\n}\n\n// wrapHandler wraps a handler with common middleware.\nfunc (s *Server) wrapHandler(h http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc {\n\treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\tstart := time.Now()\n\n\t\t// Track in-flight request\n\t\ts.requestTracker.Start()\n\t\tdefer s.requestTracker.End()\n\n\t\t// Log request\n\t\ts.logger.WithFields(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"method\": r.Method,\n\t\t\t\"path\":   r.URL.Path,\n\t\t}).Debug(\"incoming request\")\n\n\t\t// Add CORS headers for browser clients\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\", \"*\")\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\", \"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\")\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\", \"Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\")\n\n\t\t// Handle preflight\n\t\tif r.Method == http.MethodOptions {\n\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Verify auth for non-public endpoints\n\t\tif !s.isPublicPath(r.URL.Path) {\n\t\t\tcred, err := s.verifyAuthAndGetCredential(r)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\ts.writeError(w, \"AccessDenied\", \"Invalid credentials\", 403)\n\t\t\t\ts.metrics.IncRequestsTotal(\"auth\", 403)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// Check ACL for the request. For list operations the URL path has no\n\t\t\t// key component, so fall back to the ?prefix query param so that ACL\n\t\t\t// prefix restrictions are enforced correctly against the listed prefix.\n\t\t\tbucket, key := s.extractBucketAndKey(r)\n\t\t\tif key == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tkey = r.URL.Query().Get(\"prefix\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif err := CheckACL(cred, bucket, key); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\ts.writeError(w, \"AccessDenied\", \"Access Denied\", 403)\n\t\t\t\ts.metrics.IncRequestsTotal(\"acl\", 403)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Use a response writer wrapper to capture status code\n\t\trw := &responseWriter{ResponseWriter: w, statusCode: http.StatusOK}\n\n\t\th(rw, r)\n\n\t\t// Record metrics\n\t\tduration := time.Since(start)\n\t\ts.metrics.IncRequestsTotal(r.Method, rw.statusCode)\n\t\ts.metrics.RecordRequestDuration(r.Method, duration)\n\n\t\t// Log completed request\n\t\ts.logger.WithFields(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"method\":      r.Method,\n\t\t\t\"path\":        r.URL.Path,\n\t\t\t\"status\":      rw.statusCode,\n\t\t\t\"duration_ms\": duration.Milliseconds(),\n\t\t}).Info(\"request completed\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// responseWriter wraps http.ResponseWriter to capture the status code.\ntype responseWriter struct {\n\thttp.ResponseWriter\n\tstatusCode int\n}\n\n// WriteHeader captures the status code.\nfunc (rw *responseWriter) WriteHeader(code int) {\n\trw.statusCode = code\n\trw.ResponseWriter.WriteHeader(code)\n}\n\n// isPublicPath checks if a path is public (no auth required).\nfunc (s *Server) isPublicPath(path string) bool {\n\treturn path == \"/healthz\" || path == \"/readyz\"\n}\n\n// verifyAuthAndGetCredential validates AWS SigV4 authentication and returns the credential.\nfunc (s *Server) verifyAuthAndGetCredential(r *http.Request) (*config.Credential, error) {\n\t// Create auth with all credentials\n\tauth := NewSigV4AuthWithCredentials(s.config.Credentials, s.config.B2Region)\n\n\t// Check for query-based auth (presigned URLs)\n\tif r.URL.Query().Get(\"X-Amz-Credential\") != \"\" {\n\t\treturn auth.VerifyQueryAuth(r)\n\t}\n\n\t// Check for header-based auth\n\tauthHeader := r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\tif authHeader == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingAuthHeader\n\t}\n\n\t// Pass nil body — buildCanonicalRequest uses x-amz-content-sha256 header\n\t// when present (which covers all AWS SDK/CLI requests), falling back to\n\t// sha256(body) for requests without that header.\n\treturn auth.VerifyRequest(r, nil)\n}\n\n// extractBucketAndKey extracts bucket and key from the request URL.\nfunc (s *Server) extractBucketAndKey(r *http.Request) (bucket, key string) {\n\tpath := r.URL.Path\n\t// Remove leading slash\n\tpath = strings.TrimPrefix(path, \"/\")\n\n\t// Check for virtual-hosted-style (bucket in host)\n\t// For path-style: /bucket/key\n\tparts := strings.SplitN(path, \"/\", 2)\n\tif len(parts) >= 1 {\n\t\tbucket = parts[0]\n\t}\n\tif len(parts) >= 2 {\n\t\tkey = parts[1]\n\t\t// URL decode the key (DuckDB httpfs encodes special chars like = as %3D)\n\t\tif decoded, err := url.PathUnescape(key); err == nil {\n\t\t\tkey = decoded\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Use configured bucket if empty\n\tif bucket == \"\" {\n\t\tbucket = s.config.Bucket\n\t}\n\n\treturn bucket, key\n}\n\n// writeError writes an S3 error response.\nfunc (s *Server) writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n\tvar codeBuf, msgBuf bytes.Buffer\n\txml.EscapeText(&codeBuf, []byte(code))\n\txml.EscapeText(&msgBuf, []byte(message))\n\tfmt.Fprintf(w, `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`+\"\\n<Error>\\n  <Code>%s</Code>\\n  <Message>%s</Message>\\n</Error>\",\n\t\tcodeBuf.String(), msgBuf.String())\n}\n\n// GenerateDEK generates a new DEK (exposed for handlers).\nfunc (s *Server) GenerateDEK() ([]byte, error) {\n\treturn crypto.GenerateDEK()\n}\n\n// GenerateIV generates a new IV (exposed for handlers).\nfunc (s *Server) GenerateIV() ([]byte, error) {\n\treturn crypto.GenerateIV()\n}\n\n// WaitForInFlightRequests waits for all in-flight requests to complete.\nfunc (s *Server) WaitForInFlightRequests() {\n\ts.requestTracker.Wait()\n}\n\n// InFlightRequestCount returns the current number of in-flight requests.\nfunc (s *Server) InFlightRequestCount() int64 {\n\treturn s.requestTracker.Count()\n}\n\n// handlePresign generates a pre-signed URL for sharing encrypted files.\n// POST /admin/presign\n// Body: {\"bucket\": \"my-bucket\", \"key\": \"path/to/file.parquet\", \"expires_in\": \"1h\", \"content_disposition\": \"attachment; filename=\\\"file.parquet\\\"\"}\nfunc (s *Server) handlePresign(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif r.Method != http.MethodPost {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify auth\n\tcred, err := s.verifyAuthAndGetCredential(r)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\ts.writeError(w, \"AccessDenied\", \"Invalid credentials\", 403)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse request body\n\tvar req struct {\n\t\tBucket             string `json:\"bucket\"`\n\t\tKey                string `json:\"key\"`\n\t\tExpiresIn          string `json:\"expires_in\"`\n\t\tContentDisposition string `json:\"content_disposition\"`\n\t\tRange              string `json:\"range\"`\n\t}\n\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Invalid request body: %v\", err), http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Use configured bucket if not specified\n\tbucket := req.Bucket\n\tif bucket == \"\" {\n\t\tbucket = s.config.Bucket\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate required fields\n\tif req.Key == \"\" {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"key is required\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Check ACL for the request\n\tif err := CheckACL(cred, bucket, req.Key); err != nil {\n\t\ts.writeError(w, \"AccessDenied\", \"Access Denied\", 403)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse expiration (default 1 hour)\n\texpiration := presign.DefaultExpiration\n\tif req.ExpiresIn != \"\" {\n\t\texpiration, err = presign.ParseExpiration(req.ExpiresIn)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Invalid expires_in: %v\", err), http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Build options\n\tvar opts []presign.Option\n\tif req.ContentDisposition != \"\" {\n\t\topts = append(opts, presign.WithContentDisposition(req.ContentDisposition))\n\t}\n\tif req.Range != \"\" {\n\t\topts = append(opts, presign.WithRange(req.Range))\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate pre-signed URL\n\tshareURL, err := s.presigner.GenerateURL(bucket, req.Key, expiration, opts...)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate URL: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Return response\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"url\":       shareURL,\n\t\t\"expires_in\": presign.FormatExpiration(expiration),\n\t\t\"expires_at\": time.Now().Add(expiration).UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),\n\t})\n}\n\n// handleShare serves decrypted content from a pre-signed URL token.\n// GET /share/<token>\nfunc (s *Server) handleShare(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif r.Method != http.MethodGet {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Extract token from path\n\ttokenStr := presign.ParseTokenFromPath(r.URL.Path)\n\tif tokenStr == \"\" {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Missing token\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify token\n\ttoken, err := s.presigner.VerifyToken(tokenStr)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tif errors.Is(err, presign.ErrExpiredToken) {\n\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Link expired\", http.StatusGone)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tif errors.Is(err, presign.ErrInvalidSignature) {\n\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Invalid link\", http.StatusForbidden)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Invalid token\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Get object metadata\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\tinfo, err := s.backend.Head(ctx, token.Bucket, token.Key)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Object not found: %v\", err), http.StatusNotFound)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if object is ARMOR-encrypted\n\tif !info.IsARMOREncrypted {\n\t\t// Serve non-ARMOR objects directly (passthrough)\n\t\tbody, _, err := s.backend.Get(ctx, token.Bucket, token.Key)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get object: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tdefer body.Close()\n\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", info.Size))\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", info.ContentType)\n\t\tif token.ContentDisposition != \"\" {\n\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Disposition\", token.ContentDisposition)\n\t\t}\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tio.Copy(w, body)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse ARMOR metadata\n\tarmorMeta, ok := backend.ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Failed to parse object metadata\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Get the MEK for this object\n\tmek, err := s.keyManager.GetMEKByID(armorMeta.KeyID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Failed to get decryption key\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Unwrap DEK\n\tdek, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(mek, armorMeta.WrappedDEK)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Failed to unwrap DEK\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Create decryptor\n\tdecryptor, err := crypto.NewDecryptor(dek, armorMeta.IV, armorMeta.BlockSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Failed to create decryptor\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Set response headers\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", armorMeta.PlaintextSize))\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", armorMeta.ContentType)\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n\tif token.ContentDisposition != \"\" {\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Disposition\", token.ContentDisposition)\n\t}\n\n\t// Handle range request if specified in token or header\n\trangeHeader := token.Range\n\tif rangeHeader == \"\" {\n\t\trangeHeader = r.Header.Get(\"Range\")\n\t}\n\n\tif rangeHeader != \"\" {\n\t\ts.handleShareRangeRequest(w, r, token, decryptor, armorMeta, rangeHeader)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Full object download\n\ts.handleShareFullObject(w, r, token, decryptor, armorMeta)\n}\n\n// handleShareFullObject handles full object downloads for share endpoint.\nfunc (s *Server) handleShareFullObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, token *presign.Token, decryptor *crypto.Decryptor, armorMeta *backend.ARMORMetadata) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\tblockSize := armorMeta.BlockSize\n\tblockCount := int(crypto.ComputeBlockCount(armorMeta.PlaintextSize, blockSize))\n\tplaintextSize := armorMeta.PlaintextSize\n\n\t// Calculate offsets\n\thmacTableOffset := crypto.HeaderSize + plaintextSize\n\thmacTableSize := int64(blockCount) * crypto.HMACSize\n\n\t// 1. Prefetch HMAC table\n\thmacBody, err := s.backend.GetRange(ctx, token.Bucket, token.Key, hmacTableOffset, hmacTableSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to fetch HMAC table: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\thmacTable, err := io.ReadAll(hmacBody)\n\thmacBody.Close()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read HMAC table: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// 2. Stream data from Cloudflare\n\tstreamSize := crypto.HeaderSize + plaintextSize\n\tdataBody, err := s.backend.GetRange(ctx, token.Bucket, token.Key, 0, streamSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get object stream: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tdefer dataBody.Close()\n\n\t// 3. Read and discard header\n\theaderBuf := make([]byte, crypto.HeaderSize)\n\tif _, err := io.ReadFull(dataBody, headerBuf); err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read header: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// 4. Write status before streaming\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\n\t// 5. Stream decrypt\n\tencryptedBuf := make([]byte, blockSize)\n\tfor blockIndex := 0; blockIndex < blockCount; blockIndex++ {\n\t\tremaining := plaintextSize - int64(blockIndex)*int64(blockSize)\n\t\tactualBlockSize := int(min64(int64(blockSize), remaining))\n\n\t\tencryptedBuf = encryptedBuf[:actualBlockSize]\n\t\tn, err := io.ReadFull(dataBody, encryptedBuf)\n\t\tif err != nil && err != io.EOF && err != io.ErrUnexpectedEOF {\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tif n == 0 {\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t\tencryptedBuf = encryptedBuf[:n]\n\n\t\t// Verify HMAC\n\t\thmacOffset := blockIndex * crypto.HMACSize\n\t\tif hmacOffset+crypto.HMACSize > len(hmacTable) {\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\texpectedHMAC := hmacTable[hmacOffset : hmacOffset+crypto.HMACSize]\n\n\t\tmac := hmac.New(sha256.New, decryptor.HMACKey())\n\t\tindexBytes := make([]byte, 4)\n\t\tbinary.BigEndian.PutUint32(indexBytes, uint32(blockIndex))\n\t\tmac.Write(indexBytes)\n\t\tmac.Write(encryptedBuf)\n\t\tcomputed := mac.Sum(nil)\n\n\t\tif !hmac.Equal(computed, expectedHMAC) {\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Decrypt\n\t\tdecrypted := make([]byte, n)\n\t\tctr := makeCounter(armorMeta.IV, uint32(blockIndex))\n\t\tstream := cipher.NewCTR(decryptor.CipherBlock(), ctr)\n\t\tstream.XORKeyStream(decrypted, encryptedBuf)\n\n\t\t// Write to client\n\t\tw.Write(decrypted)\n\t}\n}\n\n// handleShareRangeRequest handles range requests for share endpoint.\nfunc (s *Server) handleShareRangeRequest(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, token *presign.Token, decryptor *crypto.Decryptor, armorMeta *backend.ARMORMetadata, rangeHeader string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\tplaintextSize := armorMeta.PlaintextSize\n\n\t// Parse range header\n\tstart, end, err := parseRangeHeader(rangeHeader, plaintextSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Invalid range\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Translate range to encrypted blocks\n\ttranslation, err := crypto.TranslateRange(start, end, plaintextSize, armorMeta.BlockSize, crypto.HeaderSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to translate range: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Fetch encrypted blocks and HMAC table in parallel\n\tvar encrypted, hmacTable []byte\n\n\tg, gctx := errgroup.WithContext(ctx)\n\n\tg.Go(func() error {\n\t\tencryptedBody, err := s.backend.GetRange(gctx, token.Bucket, token.Key, translation.DataOffset, translation.DataLength)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to fetch encrypted blocks: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tdefer encryptedBody.Close()\n\n\t\tencrypted, err = io.ReadAll(encryptedBody)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read encrypted blocks: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn nil\n\t})\n\n\tg.Go(func() error {\n\t\thmacBody, err := s.backend.GetRange(gctx, token.Bucket, token.Key, translation.HMACOffset, translation.HMACLength)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to fetch HMAC table: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tdefer hmacBody.Close()\n\n\t\thmacTable, err = io.ReadAll(hmacBody)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read HMAC table: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn nil\n\t})\n\n\tif err := g.Wait(); err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Decrypt range\n\tplaintext, err := decryptor.DecryptRange(encrypted, hmacTable, start, end, plaintextSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to decrypt range: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Set response headers\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", len(plaintext)))\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Range\", fmt.Sprintf(\"bytes %d-%d/%d\", start, end, plaintextSize))\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusPartialContent)\n\tw.Write(plaintext)\n}\n\nfunc min64(a, b int64) int64 {\n\tif a < b {\n\t\treturn a\n\t}\n\treturn b\n}\n\n// makeCounter creates a 16-byte counter value from the IV and block index.\nfunc makeCounter(iv []byte, blockIndex uint32) []byte {\n\tcounter := make([]byte, 16)\n\tcopy(counter[0:12], iv[0:12])\n\tbinary.BigEndian.PutUint32(counter[12:16], blockIndex)\n\treturn counter\n}\n\n// parseRangeHeader parses a Range header like \"bytes=0-1023\".\nfunc parseRangeHeader(header string, totalSize int64) (start, end int64, err error) {\n\tif !strings.HasPrefix(header, \"bytes=\") {\n\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid range format\")\n\t}\n\n\trangeSpec := strings.TrimPrefix(header, \"bytes=\")\n\n\tif strings.Contains(rangeSpec, \",\") {\n\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"multiple ranges not supported\")\n\t}\n\n\tparts := strings.Split(rangeSpec, \"-\")\n\tif len(parts) != 2 {\n\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid range format\")\n\t}\n\n\tif parts[0] == \"\" {\n\t\t// Suffix range: -500 means last 500 bytes\n\t\tsuffix, err := strconv.ParseInt(parts[1], 10, 64)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn 0, 0, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tstart = totalSize - suffix\n\t\tend = totalSize - 1\n\t} else {\n\t\tstart, err = strconv.ParseInt(parts[0], 10, 64)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn 0, 0, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tif parts[1] == \"\" {\n\t\t\tend = totalSize - 1\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tend, err = strconv.ParseInt(parts[1], 10, 64)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn 0, 0, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif start < 0 || start >= totalSize || end < start || end >= totalSize {\n\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"range out of bounds\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn start, end, nil\n}\n\n// handleB2Keys handles B2 application key management.\n// GET: List keys\n// POST: Create a new key\nfunc (s *Server) handleB2Keys(w configuration from environment variables.\n101\tfunc Load() (*Config, error) {\n102\t\tcfg := &Config{\n103\t\t\tListen:      getEnv(\"ARMOR_LISTEN\", \"0.0.0.0:9000\"),\n104\t\t\tAdminListen: == \"true\"\n138\t\n139\t\t// Master encryption key (required)\n140\t\tmekHex := os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_MEK\")\n141\t\tif mekHex == \"\" {\n142\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ARMOR_MEK is required\")\n143\t\t}\n144\t\tvar err error\n145\t\tcfg.MEK, err = hex.DecodeString(mekHex)\n146\t\tif err != nil {\n147\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ARMOR_MEK must be hex-encoded: %w\", \"0\"\n205\t\tcfg.ManifestPrefix = getEnv(\"ARMOR_MANIFEST_PREFIX\", \".armor/manifest\")\n206\t\tcfg.ManifestCompactionInterval = getEnvInt(\"ARMOR_MANIFEST_COMPACTION_INTERVAL\", os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_PRESIGN_SECRET\")\n211\t\tif presignSecretHex != \"\" {\n212\t\t\tcfg.PresignSecret, err = hex.DecodeString(presignSecretHex)\n213\t\t\tif err != nil {\n214\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ARMOR_PRESIGN_SECRET must be hex-encoded: %w\", err)\n215\t\t\t}\n216\t\t\tif len(cfg.PresignSecret) < 32 {\n217\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ARMOR_PRESIGN_SECRET must be at least 32 bytes (64 hex chars)\")\n218\t\t\t}\n219\t\t} else {\n220\t\t\t// Use the auth secret key as the presign secret if not specified\n221\t\t\tcfg.PresignSecret = []byte(cfg.AuthSecretKey)\n222\t\t}\n223\t\tcfg.PresignBaseURL = os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_PRESIGN_BASE_URL\")\n224\t\tif cfg.PresignBaseURL == \"\" {\n225\t\t\t// Default to /share path on the main listener\n226\t\t\tcfg.PresignBaseURL = \"/share\"\n227\t\t}\n228\t\n229\t\t// Load named keys (ARMOR_MEK_<NAME>)\n230\t\tcfg.NamedKeys = make(map[string][]byte)\n231\t\tfor _, env := range os.Environ() {\n232\t\t\t// Look for ARMOR_MEK_<NAME> pattern\n233\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(env, \"ARMOR_MEK_\") {\n234\t\t\t\tparts := strings.SplitN(env, \"=\", 2)\n235\t\t\t\tif len(parts) != 2 {\n236\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n237\t\t\t\t}\n238\t\t\t\tname := strings.TrimPrefix(parts[0], \"ARMOR_MEK_\")\n239\t\t\t\tname = strings.ToLower(name)\n240\t\t\t\tif name == \"\" {\n241\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n242\t\t\t\t}\n243\t\t\t\t// Decode hex MEK\n244\t\t\t\tmek, err := hex.DecodeString(parts[1])\n245\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n246\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ARMOR_MEK_%s must be hex-encoded: %w\", name, err)\n247\t\t\t\t}\n248\t\t\t\tif len(mek) != 32 {\n249\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ARMOR_MEK_%s must be 32 bytes (64 hex chars), got %d bytes\", name, len(mek))\n250\t\t\t\t}\n251\t\t\t\tcfg.NamedKeys[name] = mek\n252\t\t\t}\n253\t\t}\n254\t\n255\t\t// Load key routes (ARMOR_KEY_ROUTES)\n256\t\tif routesStr := os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_KEY_ROUTES\"); routesStr != \"\" {\n257\t\t\troutes, err := parseKeyRoutes(routesStr)\n258\t\t\tif err != nil {\n259\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ARMOR_KEY_ROUTES: %w\", err)\n260\t\t\t}\n261\t\t\tcfg.KeyRoutes = routes\n262\t\t}\n263\t\n264\t\t// Dashboard authentication configuration\n265\t\tcfg.DashboardUser = os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_DASHBOARD_USER\")\n266\t\tcfg.DashboardPass = os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_DASHBOARD_PASS\")\n267\t\tcfg.DashboardToken = os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_DASHBOARD_TOKEN\")\n268\t\n269\t\treturn cfg, nil\n270\t}\n271\t\n272\tfunc getEnv(key, defaultVal string) string {\n273\t\tif val := os.Getenv(key); val != \"\" {\n274\t\t\treturn val\n275\t\t}\n276\t\treturn defaultVal\n277\t}\n278\t\n279\tfunc getEnvInt(key string, defaultVal int) int {\n280\t\tif val := os.Getenv(key); val != \"\" {\n281\t\t\tif i, err := strconv.Atoi(val); err == nil {\n282\t\t\t\treturn i\n283\t\t\t}\n284\t\t}\n285\t\treturn defaultVal\n286\t}\n287\t\n288\tfunc generateRandomKey(length int) string {\n289\t\tb := make([]byte, length)\n290\t\tif _, err := rand.Read(b); err != nil {\n291\t\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"failed to generate random key: %v\", err))\n292\t\t}\n293\t\treturn hex.EncodeToString(b)\n294\t}\n295\t\n296\t// parseKeyRoutes parses a key routes string.\n297\t// Format: \"prefix1=key1,prefix2=key2,*=default\"\n298\t// The * prefix is a catch-all that maps to the default key.\n299\tfunc parseKeyRoutes(routesStr string) ([]KeyRoute, error) {\n300\t\tif routesStr == \"\" {\n301\t\t\treturn nil, nil\n302\t\t}\n303\t\n304\t\tvar routes []KeyRoute\n305\t\tparts := strings.Split(routesStr, \",\")\n306\t\n307\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n308\t\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n309\t\t\tif part == \"\" {\n310\t\t\t\tcontinue\n311\t\t\t}\n312\t\n313\t\t\tkv := strings.SplitN(part, \"=\", 2)\n314\t\t\tif len(kv) != 2 {\n315\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid route format %q (expected prefix=keyname)\", part)\n316\t\t\t}\n317\t\n318\t\t\tprefix := strings.TrimSpace(kv[0])\n319\t\t\tkeyName := strings.TrimSpace(kv[1])\n320\t\n321\t\t\tif prefix == \"\" || keyName == \"\" {\n322\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid route %q (empty prefix or key name)\", part)\n323\t\t\t}\n324\t\n325\t\t\t// Handle wildcard - it maps to default key\n326\t\t\tif prefix == \"*\" {\n327\t\t\t\tprefix = \"\"\n328\t\t\t}\n329\t\n330\t\t\troutes = append(routes, KeyRoute{\n331\t\t\t\tPrefix:  prefix,\n332\t\t\t\tKeyName: keyName,\n333\t\t\t})\n334\t\t}\n335\t\n336\t\treturn routes, nil\n337\t}\n338\t\n339\t// loadNamedCredentials loads additional named credentials from environment variables.\n340\t// Format: ARMOR_AUTH_<NAME>_ACCESS_KEY, ARMOR_AUTH_<NAME>_SECRET_KEY, err))\n\t}\n\treturn hex.EncodeToString(b)\n}\n\n// parseKeyRoutes parses a key routes string.\n// Format: \"prefix1=key1,prefix2=key2,*=default\"\n// The * prefix is a catch-all that maps to the default key.\nfunc parseKeyRoutes(routesStr string) ([]KeyRoute, error) {\n\tif routesStr == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, nil\n\t}\n\n\tvar routes []KeyRoute\n\tparts := strings.Split(routesStr, {\n\t\t\tprefix = \"\"\n\t\t}\n\n\t\troutes = append(routes, KeyRoute{\n\t\t\tPrefix:  prefix,\n\t\t\tKeyName: keyName,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\treturn routes, nil\n}\n\n// loadNamedCredentials loads parseACL parses an ACL string into ACL entries.\n// Format: \"bucket1:prefix1,bucket2:prefix2,bucket3:*\"\n// A bucket of \"*\" means all buckets.\n// A prefix of \"*\" or \"\" means any prefix within the bucket.\nfunc parseACL(aclStr string) ([]ACLEntry, error) {\n\tif aclStr == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, nil\n\t}\n\n\tvar entries []ACLEntry\n\tparts := strings.Split(aclStr, \",\")\n\n\tfor _, part := range parts {\n\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n\t\tif part == \"\" {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Split bucket:prefix\n\t\tkv := strings.SplitN(part, \":\", 2)\n\t\tif len(kv) != 2 Create an Ingress resource that routes to the admin-api port:\n35\t\n36\t```yaml\n37\tapiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1\n38\tkind: Ingress\n39\tmetadata:\n40\t  name: armor-dashboard\n41\t  annotations:\n42\t    # Use your Ingress controller annotations\n43\t    # For NGINX:\n44\t    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/rewrite-target: /\n45\t    # For Traefik:\n46\t    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: websecure\n47\tspec:\n48\t  ingressClassName: nginx\n49\t  rules:\n50\t  - host: armor-dashboard.example.com\n51\t    http:\n52\t      paths:\n53\t      - path: /\n54\t        pathType: Prefix\n55\t        backend:\n56\t          service:\n57\t            name: armor\n58\t            port:\n59\t              number: 9001\n60\t  tls:\n61\t  - hosts:\n62\t    - armor-dashboard.example.com\n63\t    secretName: armor-dashboard-tls\n64\t```\n65\t\n66\t## Dashboard Authentication\n67\t\n68\tThe dashboard supports two authentication methods to protect against unauthorized access.\n69\t\n70\t### HTTP Basic Authentication\n71\t\n72\tSet username and password via environment variables:\n73\t\n74\t```yaml\n75\tenv:\n76\t  - name: ARMOR_DASHBOARD_USER\n77\t    value: \"admin\"\n78\t  - name: ARMOR_DASHBOARD_PASS\n79\t    value: \"secure-password-here\"\n80\t```\n81\t\n82\tWhen accessing the dashboard, provide credentials:\n83\t```bash\n84\t# Using curl\n85\tcurl -u admin:secure-password-here http://localhost:9001/dashboard\n86\t\n87\t# Browser will prompt for username/password\n88\t```\n89\t\n90\t### Bearer Token Authentication\n91\t\n92\tAlternatively, use a bearer token:\n93\t\n94\t```yaml\n95\tenv:\n96\t  - name: ARMOR_DASHBOARD_TOKEN\n97\t    value: \"your-secure-token-here\"\n98\t```\n99\t\n100\tWhen accessing the dashboard, provide the token in the Authorization header:\n101\t```bash\n102\tcurl -H \"Authorization: Bearer your-secure-token-here\" http://localhost:9001/dashboard\n103\t```\n104\t\n105\t### Security Notes\n106\t\n107\t- If neither authentication method is configured, the dashboard is open to anyone with network access to the admin port\n108\t- Health check endpoints (`/healthz`, `/readyz`) remain unauthenticated for Kubernetes probes\n109\t- For production deployments, always enable authentication or use network policies to restrict access\n110\t- Consider using a strong random password or token generated with `openssl rand -hex 32`\n111\t\n112\t## Dashboard Features\n113\t\n114\t### 1. Bucket Browsing\n115\t\n116\tBrowse objects in your encrypted bucket with prefix-based navigation:\n117\t\n118\t- **Breadcrumb navigation** - Shows current path and allows quick navigation to parent folders\n119\t- **Prefix filtering** - Navigate into folders by clicking on them\n120\t- **Object listing** - Shows all objects in the current prefix\n121\t\n122\t### 2. Encryption Status Visualization\n123\t\n124\tSee at a glance which objects are encrypted:\n125\t\n126\t- **ARMOR badge** - Green badge on encrypted objects showing the key ID used\n127\t- **Key ID display** - Shows which MEK (default, sensitive, archive, etc.) was used\n128\t- **Plain text indicator** - Shows \"plain\" for unencrypted objects\n129\t\n130\tExample display:\n131\t```\n132\t📄 data/sensor-readings.parquet  [ARMOR [sensitive]]  1.2 GB\n133\t📄 config.json                  [plain]               2.3 KB\n134\t📁 logs/                                              —\n135\t```\n136\t\n137\t### 3. Cache Statistics\n138\t\n139\tMonitor the effectiveness of the metadata cache:\n140\t\n141\t- **Cache Hit Rate** - Percentage of requests served from cache vs. B2 API calls\n142\t- **Cache Hits / Misses** - Absolute counts for monitoring\n143\t- Higher hit rates = fewer B2 API calls = lower costs (after May 2026)\n144\t\n145\t### 4. System Metrics\n146\t\n147\tReal-time operational metrics:\n148\t\n149\t- **Total Requests** - Cumulative request count\n150\t- **Bytes Uploaded** - Plaintext bytes received from clients\n151\t- **Bytes Downloaded** - Plaintext bytes served to clients\n152\t- **Uptime** - Server uptime duration\n153\t- **Canary Status** - Health status from the canary integrity monitor\n154\t\n155\t### 5. Object Details\n156\t\n157\tClick any object to view detailed metadata:\n158\t\n159\t```json\n160\t{\n161\t  \"key\": \"data/sensor-readings.parquet\",\n162\t  \"size\": 128974848,\n163\t  \"content_type\": \"application/octet-stream\",\n164\t  \"etag\": \"a1b2c3d4e5f6\",\n165\t  \"last_modified\": \"2026-05-07T12:00:00Z\",\n166\t  \"is_armor\": true,\n167\t  \"armor\": {\n168\t    \"plaintext_size\": 128974848,\n169\t    \"block_size\": 65536,\n170\t    \"key_id\": \"sensitive\",\n171\t    \"iv\": \"0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef\",\n172\t    \"wrapped_dek\": \"base64encodedwrappedkey\",\n173\t    \"sha256\": \"hexdigestofplaintext\"\n174\t  }\n175\t}\n176\t```\n177\t\n178\t### 6. JSON Metrics Endpoint\n179\t\n180\tFor programmatic access and monitoring integrations:\n181\t\n182\t```bash\n183\tcurl http://localhost:9001/dashboard/metrics\n184\t```\n185\t\n186\tReturns:\n187\t```json\n188\t{\n189\t  \"requests_total\": 15234,\n190\t  \"requests_in_flight\": 3,\n191\t  \"bytes_uploaded\": 5368709120,\n192\t  \"bytes_downloaded\": 21474836480,\n193\t  \"bytes_fetched_from_b2\": 12884901888,\n194\t  \"range_reads_total\": 456,\n195\t  \"range_bytes_saved\": 10737418240,\n196\t  \"cache_hits\": 12000,\n197\t  \"cache_misses\": 3234,\n198\t  \"key_wrap_ops\": 150,\n199\t  \"key_unwrap_ops\": 15000,\n200\t  \"canary_checks\": 240,\n201\t  \"canary_failures\": 0,\n202\t  \"active_multipart\": 0,\n203\t  \"provenance_entries\": 150,\n204\t  \"uptime_seconds\": 86400.0\n205\t}\n206\t```\n207\t\n208\t## Dashboard Endpoints\n209\t\n210\t| Endpoint | Method | Description |\n211\t|----------|--------|-------------|\n212\t| `/dashboard` | GET | Main dashboard UI (HTML) |\n213\t| `/dashboard?prefix=data/` | GET | Browse with prefix filter |\n214\t| `/dashboard/object?key=path/to/file` | GET | Object details (JSON) |\n215\t| `/dashboard/metrics` | GET | All metrics (JSON) |\n216\t\n217\t## Performance Considerations\n218\t\n219\t- **Listing performance**: The dashboard fetches up to 1000 objects per request. For buckets with many objects, use prefix navigation to narrow the scope.\n220\t- **Cache warm-up**: After ARMOR restarts, the cache hit rate will be low until metadata is loaded. The manifest index (if enabled) will warm the cache on startup.\n221\t- **No auth required**: The dashboard is currently accessible to anyone with network access to the admin port. Secure it appropriately in production.\n222\t\n223\t## Troubleshooting\n224\t\n225\t### Dashboard shows \"Failed to list objects\"\n226\t\n227\t1. Check B2 connectivity: `kubectl exec deploy/armor -- curl -s localhost:9001/healthz`\n228\t2. Verify credentials: Check B2 credentials in the `armor-secrets` Secret\n229\t3. Check logs: `kubectl logs -f deploy/armor`\n230\t\n231\t### Can't access dashboard externally\n232\t\n233\t1. Verify Service exists: `kubectl get svc armor`\n234\t2. Check port-forward is running: `kubectl port-forward svc/armor 9001:9001`\n235\t3. For Ingress: Verify Ingress controller is running and DNS is configured\n236\t\n237\t### Cache hit rate is 0%\n238\t\n239\tThis is normal for:\n240\t- Fresh ARMOR deployments (cache not yet warmed)\n241\t- Buckets with poor access locality (random object access)\n242\t- After cache clears (TTL expiration)\n243\t\n244\tThe cache will warm up as objects are accessed. For frequently-accessed objects (e.g., Parquet footers, hot data), you should see 60-90% hit rates during normal operation.\n245\t\n246\t## Future Enhancements\n247\t\n248\tPotential improvements for the dashboard:\n249\t\n250\t- **Historical metrics** - Graph metrics over time using Prometheus/Grafana\n251\t- **Object search** - Full-text search across object keys\n252\t- **Bulk operations** - Select multiple objects for deletion or re-encryption\n253\t- **Key management UI** - Visual interface for key rotation and multi-key configuration (key rotation trigger already implemented)\n254\t- **Upload/download** - Direct file upload and download through the web UI\n255\t- **Real-time updates** - WebSocket updates for live metrics and new objects\n256\t\n257\t## Contributing\n258\t\n259\tThe dashboard is implemented in Go with no external frontend dependencies. The HTML/CSS/JavaScript is embedded directly in the Go code for simplicity. To modify the UI:\n260\t\n261\t1. Edit `internal/dashboard/dashboard.go`\n262\t2. Update the `dashboardHTML` template constant\n263\t3. Rebuild and redeploy: `docker build -t armor:local . && kubectl rollout restart deployment/armor`\n264\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d73a4da1-748d-4136-b454-bd4edd15fa81","uuid":"15792370-9482-423a-bd5f-773cb48eeda4","timestamp":"2026-05-08T13:46:35.709Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/docs/dashboard.md","content":"# ARMOR Web Dashboard\n\nThe ARMOR web dashboard provides a visual interface for browsing encrypted buckets, viewing encryption status, and monitoring cache statistics.\n\n## Accessing the Dashboard\n\n### Local Development\n\nWhen running ARMOR locally, the dashboard is available on the admin port (default `127.0.0.1:9001`):\n\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:9001/dashboard\n```\n\nOr open in a browser:\n```\nhttp://localhost:9001/dashboard\n```\n\n### Kubernetes Deployment\n\nThe dashboard is exposed via the admin-api service on port 9001:\n\n```bash\n# Port-forward to access locally\nkubectl port-forward svc/armor 9001:9001\n\n# Then open in browser\nopen http://localhost:9001/dashboard\n```\n\n### External Access via Ingress\n\nFor production deployments, you can expose the dashboard externally using Kubernetes Ingress. Create an Ingress resource that routes to the admin-api port:\n\n```yaml\napiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1\nkind: Ingress\nmetadata:\n  name: armor-dashboard\n  annotations:\n    # Use your Ingress controller annotations\n    # For NGINX:\n    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/rewrite-target: /\n    # For Traefik:\n    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: websecure\nspec:\n  ingressClassName: nginx\n  rules:\n  - host: armor-dashboard.example.com\n    http:\n      paths:\n      - path: /\n        pathType: Prefix\n        backend:\n          service:\n            name: armor\n            port:\n              number: 9001\n  tls:\n  - hosts:\n    - armor-dashboard.example.com\n    secretName: armor-dashboard-tls\n```\n\n## Dashboard Authentication\n\nThe dashboard supports two authentication methods to protect against unauthorized access.\n\n### HTTP Basic Authentication\n\nSet username and password via environment variables:\n\n```yaml\nenv:\n  - name: ARMOR_DASHBOARD_USER\n    value: \"admin\"\n  - name: ARMOR_DASHBOARD_PASS\n    value: \"secure-password-here\"\n```\n\nWhen accessing the dashboard, provide credentials:\n```bash\n# Using curl\ncurl -u [REDACTED:curl-auth-user] http://localhost:9001/dashboard\n\n# Browser will prompt for username/password\n```\n\n### Bearer Token Authentication\n\nAlternatively, use a bearer token:\n\n```yaml\nenv:\n  - name: ARMOR_DASHBOARD_TOKEN\n    value: \"your-secure-token-here\"\n```\n\nWhen accessing the dashboard, provide the token in the Authorization header:\n```bash\ncurl -H \"Authorization: Bearer your-secure-token-here\" http://localhost:9001/dashboard\n```\n\n### Security Notes\n\n- If neither authentication method is configured, the dashboard is open to anyone with network access to the admin port\n- Health check endpoints (`/healthz`, `/readyz`) remain unauthenticated for Kubernetes probes\n- For production deployments, always enable authentication or use network policies to restrict access\n- Consider using a strong random password or token generated with `openssl rand -hex 32`\n\n## Dashboard Features\n\n### 1. Bucket Browsing\n\nBrowse objects in your encrypted bucket with prefix-based navigation:\n\n- **Breadcrumb navigation** - Shows current path and allows quick navigation to parent folders\n- **Prefix filtering** - Navigate into folders by clicking on them\n- **Object listing** - Shows all objects in the current prefix\n\n### 2. Encryption Status Visualization\n\nSee at a glance which objects are encrypted:\n\n- **ARMOR badge** - Green badge on encrypted objects showing the key ID used\n- **Key ID display** - Shows which MEK (default, sensitive, archive, etc.) was used\n- **Plain text indicator** - Shows \"plain\" for unencrypted objects\n\nExample display:\n```\n📄 data/sensor-readings.parquet  [ARMOR [sensitive]]  1.2 GB\n📄 config.json                  [plain]               2.3 KB\n📁 logs/                                              —\n```\n\n### 3. Cache Statistics\n\nMonitor the effectiveness of the metadata cache:\n\n- **Cache Hit Rate** - Percentage of requests served from cache vs. B2 API calls\n- **Cache Hits / Misses** - Absolute counts for monitoring\n- Higher hit rates = fewer B2 API calls = lower costs (after May 2026)\n\n### 4. System Metrics\n\nReal-time operational metrics:\n\n- **Total Requests** - Cumulative request count\n- **Bytes Uploaded** - Plaintext bytes received from clients\n- **Bytes Downloaded** - Plaintext bytes served to clients\n- **Uptime** - Server uptime duration\n- **Canary Status** - Health status from the canary integrity monitor\n\n### 5. Object Details\n\nClick any object to view detailed metadata:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"key\": \"data/sensor-readings.parquet\",\n  \"size\": 128974848,\n  \"content_type\": \"application/octet-stream\",\n  \"etag\": \"a1b2c3d4e5f6\",\n  \"last_modified\": \"2026-05-07T12:00:00Z\",\n  \"is_armor\": true,\n  \"armor\": {\n    \"plaintext_size\": 128974848,\n    \"block_size\": 65536,\n    \"key_id\": \"sensitive\",\n    \"iv\": \"0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef\",\n    \"wrapped_dek\": \"base64encodedwrappedkey\",\n    \"sha256\": \"hexdigestofplaintext\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### 6. JSON Metrics Endpoint\n\nFor programmatic access and monitoring integrations:\n\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:9001/dashboard/metrics\n```\n\nReturns:\n```json\n{\n  \"requests_total\": 15234,\n  \"requests_in_flight\": 3,\n  \"bytes_uploaded\": 5368709120,\n  \"bytes_downloaded\": 21474836480,\n  \"bytes_fetched_from_b2\": 12884901888,\n  \"range_reads_total\": 456,\n  \"range_bytes_saved\": 10737418240,\n  \"cache_hits\": 12000,\n  \"cache_misses\": 3234,\n  \"key_wrap_ops\": 150,\n  \"key_unwrap_ops\": 15000,\n  \"canary_checks\": 240,\n  \"canary_failures\": 0,\n  \"active_multipart\": 0,\n  \"provenance_entries\": 150,\n  \"uptime_seconds\": 86400.0\n}\n```\n\n## Dashboard Endpoints\n\n| Endpoint | Method | Description |\n|----------|--------|-------------|\n| `/dashboard` | GET | Main dashboard UI (HTML) |\n| `/dashboard?prefix=data/` | GET | Browse with prefix filter |\n| `/dashboard/object?key=path/to/file` | GET | Object details (JSON) |\n| `/dashboard/metrics` | GET | All metrics (JSON) |\n\n## Performance Considerations\n\n- **Listing performance**: The dashboard fetches up to 1000 objects per request. For buckets with many objects, use prefix navigation to narrow the scope.\n- **Cache warm-up**: After ARMOR restarts, the cache hit rate will be low until metadata is loaded. The manifest index (if enabled) will warm the cache on startup.\n- **No auth required**: The dashboard is currently accessible to anyone with network access to the admin port. Secure it appropriately in production.\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### Dashboard shows \"Failed to list objects\"\n\n1. Check B2 connectivity: `kubectl exec deploy/armor -- curl -s localhost:9001/healthz`\n2. Verify credentials: Check B2 credentials in the `armor-secrets` Secret\n3. Check logs: `kubectl logs -f deploy/armor`\n\n### Can't access dashboard externally\n\n1. Verify Service exists: `kubectl get svc armor`\n2. Check port-forward is running: `kubectl port-forward svc/armor 9001:9001`\n3. For Ingress: Verify Ingress controller is running and DNS is configured\n\n### Cache hit rate is 0%\n\nThis is normal for:\n- Fresh ARMOR deployments (cache not yet warmed)\n- Buckets with poor access locality (random object access)\n- After cache clears (TTL expiration)\n\nThe cache will warm up as objects are accessed. For frequently-accessed objects (e.g., Parquet footers, hot data), you should see 60-90% hit rates during normal operation.\n\n## Future Enhancements\n\nPotential improvements for the dashboard:\n\n- **Historical metrics** - Graph metrics over time using Prometheus/Grafana\n- **Object search** - Full-text search across object keys\n- **Bulk operations** - Select multiple objects for deletion or re-encryption\n- **Key management UI** - Visual interface for key rotation and multi-key configuration (key rotation trigger already implemented)\n- **Upload/download** - Direct file upload and download through the web UI\n- **Real-time updates** - WebSocket updates for live metrics and new objects\n\n## Contributing\n\nThe dashboard is implemented in Go with no external frontend dependencies. The HTML/CSS/JavaScript is embedded directly in the Go code for simplicity. To modify the UI:\n\n1. Edit `internal/dashboard/dashboard.go`\n2. Update the `dashboardHTML` template constant\n3. Rebuild and redeploy: `docker build -t armor:local . && kubectl rollout restart deployment/armor`\n","numLines":264,"startLine":1,"totalLines":264}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_c6d9f0593a4b42d5adab23d3","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# 🛡️ ARMOR\n2\t\n3\t**Authenticated Range-readable Managed Object Repository**\n4\t\n5\t---\n6\t\n7\t## 🔤 What Does ARMOR Stand For?\n8\t\n9\t| Letter | Word | Meaning |\n10\t|:------:|------|---------|\n11\t| **A** | Authenticated | Every operation is cryptographically verified — envelope encryption with per-file keys, per-block HMACs, and scoped access tokens |\n12\t| **R** | Range-readable | AES-CTR block-level encryption enables random-access decryption — tools like DuckDB can query encrypted Parquet files without downloading the whole thing |\n13\t| **M** | Managed | Transparent key lifecycle — automatic DEK generation, master key wrapping, and server-side key rotation via metadata-only copies |\n14\t| **O** | Object | Built on S3-compatible object storage (Backblaze B2) with full support for multipart uploads, lifecycle rules, and object lock |\n15\t| **R** | Repository | A unified encrypted data layer — upload, download, sync, query, and share files through a single interface |\n16\t\n17\t---\n18\t\n19\t## 🎯 What Is ARMOR?\n20\t\n21\tARMOR is an **S3-compatible proxy server** that transparently encrypts and decrypts data between clients and [Backblaze B2](https://www.backblaze.com/cloud-storage). It leverages [Cloudflare's](https://www.cloudflare.com/) global edge network for zero-egress downloads.\n22\t\n23\t- 🔐 **Zero-knowledge encryption** — data is encrypted before it leaves ARMOR; B2 only stores ciphertext\n24\t- 💸 **Zero egress fees** — all downloads route through Cloudflare via the Bandwidth Alliance\n25\t- 🔍 **Seekable encryption** — AES-256-CTR with 64KB blocks enables byte-range reads on encrypted files\n26\t- 🦆 **DuckDB integration** — query encrypted Parquet files with column pruning and predicate pushdown intact\n27\t- 🪄 **Transparent operation** — any S3-compatible tool (boto3, AWS CLI, DuckDB, rclone) works unmodified\n28\t- 🔑 **Multi-key support** — different MEKs for different prefixes with automatic key routing\n29\t\n30\t---\n31\t\n32\t## 💰 Cost Model\n33\t\n34\t| Component | Cost |\n35\t|-----------|------|\n36\t| 💾 Storage | ~$6–7/TB/month |\n37\t| 📤 Egress (via Cloudflare) | $0 |\n38\t| 📡 API calls (after May 2026) | $0 |\n39\t| 🌐 Cloudflare (free plan) | $0 |\n40\t| **Total** | **~$6–7/TB/month** |\n41\t\n42\t---\n43\t\n44\t## 🏗️ Architecture\n45\t\n46\t### 📤 Upload Path (direct to B2 — ingress is free)\n47\t\n48\t```\n49\t┌──────────┐     ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────┐\n50\t│  Client   │────▶│    ARMOR     │────▶│    B2    │\n51\t│           │     │  encrypt +   │     │          │\n52\t│           │     │  upload      │     │          │\n53\t└──────────┘     └──────────────┘     └──────────┘\n54\t```\n55\t\n56\t### 📥 Download Path (through Cloudflare — egress is free)\n57\t\n58\t```\n59\t┌──────────┐     ┌────────────┐     ┌────────────┐     ┌──────────┐\n60\t│  Client   │◀───│ Cloudflare │◀───│  Cloudflare │◀───│    B2    │\n61\t│  ARMOR    │    │   Edge     │    │  PNI Link   │    │          │\n62\t│  decrypt  │    │  (cache)   │    │  (free)     │    │          │\n63\t└──────────┘     └────────────┘     └────────────┘     └──────────┘\n64\t```\n65\t\n66\t### 🦆 DuckDB Query Path (seekable decryption)\n67\t\n68\t```\n69\tDuckDB                          ARMOR FS                    Cloudflare → B2\n70\t  │                                │                              │\n71\t  ├─ read footer (last 8 bytes) ──▶├─ Range GET (encrypted) ────▶│\n72\t  │◀── decrypted footer ──────────┤◀── ciphertext ──────────────┤\n73\t  │                                │                              │\n74\t  ├─ read col_a, row group 3 ────▶├─ Range GET (3 blocks) ─────▶│\n75\t  │◀── decrypted column chunk ────┤◀── ciphertext ──────────────┤\n76\t  │                                │                              │\n77\t  └─ result set                    └                              └\n78\t```\n79\t\n80\t---\n81\t\n82\t## 🔐 Security Model\n83\t\n84\t| 🛡️ Threat | ✅ Mitigation |\n85\t|-----------|-------------|\n86\t| B2 breach | Client-side encryption — B2 only stores opaque blobs |\n87\t| Cloudflare inspection | Client-side encryption — Cloudflare only caches opaque blobs |\n88\t| Man-in-the-middle | TLS everywhere + client-side encryption |\n89\t| Key compromise | Envelope encryption — per-file DEKs limit blast radius; key rotation re-wraps without re-uploading |\n90\t| Data corruption | SHA-256 integrity hash + per-block HMACs |\n91\t| Unauthorized access | Private bucket + Cloudflare Worker auth + scoped application keys |\n92\t\n93\t---\n94\t\n95\t## 🔑 Encryption Design\n96\t\n97\t```\n98\t🔑 Master Key (MEK)\n99\t │  stored locally, never uploaded\n100\t │\n101\t └─▶ wraps ──▶ 🔑 Data Encryption Key (DEK)\n102\t                │  random per-file, wrapped copy in B2 metadata\n103\t                │\n104\t                └─▶ encrypts ──▶ 📦 File Data\n105\t                                   AES-256-CTR, 64KB blocks\n106\t                                   per-block HMAC-SHA256\n107\t                                   seekable random access\n108\t```\n109\t\n110\t---\n111\t\n112\t## 🚀 Quick Start\n113\t\n114\t### Docker\n115\t\n116\t```bash\n117\tdocker run -d \\\n118\t  -p 9000:9000 \\\n119\t  -p 9001:9001 \\\n120\t  -e ARMOR_B2_REGION=us-east-005 \\\n121\t  -e ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID=your-key-id \\\n122\t  -e ARMOR_B2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=your-key-secret \\\n123\t  -e ARMOR_BUCKET=your-bucket \\\n124\t  -e ARMOR_CF_DOMAIN=your-cf-domain.example.com \\\n125\t  -e ARMOR_MEK=$(openssl rand -hex 32) \\\n126\t  -e ARMOR_AUTH_ACCESS_KEY=my-access-key \\\n127\t  -e ARMOR_AUTH_SECRET_KEY=my-secret-key \\\n128\t  ghcr.io/jedarden/armor:latest\n129\t```\n130\t\n131\t### Client Configuration\n132\t\n133\tPoint any S3-compatible tool at ARMOR:\n134\t\n135\t```bash\n136\t# AWS CLI\n137\taws --endpoint-url http://localhost:9000 s3 cp file.txt s3://bucket/key\n138\t\n139\t# boto3 (Python)\n140\timport boto3\n141\ts3 = boto3.client('s3',\n142\t    endpoint_url='http://localhost:9000',\n143\t    aws_access_key_id='my-access-key',\n144\t    aws_secret_access_key='my-secret-key')\n145\ts3.upload_file('local.txt', 'bucket', 'key')\n146\t\n147\t# DuckDB\n148\tINSTALL httpfs;\n149\tLOAD httpfs;\n150\tSET s3_endpoint='localhost:9000';\n151\tSET s3_access_key_id='my-access-key';\n152\tSET s3_secret_access_key='my-secret-key';\n153\tSELECT * FROM read_parquet('s3://bucket/data.parquet');\n154\t```\n155\t\n156\t---\n157\t\n158\t## ⚙️ Configuration\n159\t\n160\tARMOR is configured via environment variables:\n161\t\n162\t| Variable | Required | Default | Description |\n163\t|----------|----------|---------|-------------|\n164\t| `ARMOR_LISTEN` | No | `0.0.0.0:9000` | S3 API listen address |\n165\t| `ARMOR_ADMIN_LISTEN` | No | `127.0.0.1:9001` | Admin API (key rotation, canary, audit) |\n166\t| `ARMOR_B2_REGION` | Yes | — | B2 region (e.g., `us-east-005`) |\n167\t| `ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID` | Yes | — | B2 application key ID 🛡️ ARMOR\n\n**Authenticated Range-readable Managed Object Repository**\n\n---\n\n## 🔤 What Does ARMOR Stand For?\n\n| Letter | Word | Meaning |\n|:------:|------|---------|\n| **A** | Authenticated | Every operation is cryptographically verified — envelope encryption with per-file keys, per-block HMACs, and scoped access tokens |\n| **R** | Range-readable | AES-CTR block-level encryption enables random-access decryption — tools like DuckDB can query encrypted Parquet files without downloading the whole thing |\n| **M** | Managed | Transparent key lifecycle — automatic DEK generation, master key wrapping, and server-side key rotation via metadata-only copies |\n| **O** | Object | Built on S3-compatible object storage (Backblaze B2) with full support for multipart uploads, lifecycle rules, and object lock |\n| **R** | Repository | A unified encrypted data layer — upload, download, sync, query, and share files through a single interface |\n\n---\n\n## 🎯 What Is ARMOR?\n\nARMOR is an **S3-compatible proxy server** that transparently encrypts and decrypts data between clients and [Backblaze B2](https://www.backblaze.com/cloud-storage). It leverages [Cloudflare's](https://www.cloudflare.com/) global edge network for zero-egress downloads.\n\n- 🔐 **Zero-knowledge encryption** — data is encrypted before it leaves ARMOR; B2 only stores ciphertext\n- 💸 **Zero egress fees** — all downloads route through Cloudflare via the Bandwidth Alliance\n- 🔍 **Seekable encryption** — AES-256-CTR with 64KB blocks enables byte-range reads on encrypted files\n- 🦆 **DuckDB integration** — query encrypted Parquet files with column pruning and predicate pushdown intact\n- 🪄 **Transparent operation** — any S3-compatible tool (boto3, AWS CLI, DuckDB, rclone) works unmodified\n- 🔑 **Multi-key support** — different MEKs for different prefixes with automatic key routing\n\n---\n\n## 💰 Cost Model\n\n| Component | Cost |\n|-----------|------|\n| 💾 Storage | ~$6–7/TB/month |\n| 📤 Egress (via Cloudflare) | $0 |\n| 📡 API calls (after May 2026) | $0 |\n| 🌐 Cloudflare (free plan) | $0 |\n| **Total** | **~$6–7/TB/month** |\n\n---\n\n## 🏗️ Architecture\n\n### 📤 Upload Path (direct to B2 — ingress is free)\n\n```\n┌──────────┐     ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────┐\n│  Client   │────▶│    ARMOR     │────▶│    B2    │\n│           │     │  encrypt +   │     │          │\n│           │     │  upload      │     │          │\n└──────────┘     └──────────────┘     └──────────┘\n```\n\n### 📥 Download Path (through Cloudflare — egress is free)\n\n```\n┌──────────┐     ┌────────────┐     ┌────────────┐     ┌──────────┐\n│  Client   │◀───│ Cloudflare │◀───│  Cloudflare │◀───│    B2    │\n│  ARMOR    │    │   Edge     │    │  PNI Link   │    │          │\n│  decrypt  │    │  (cache)   │    │  (free)     │    │          │\n└──────────┘     └────────────┘     └────────────┘     └──────────┘\n```\n\n### 🦆 DuckDB Query Path (seekable decryption)\n\n```\nDuckDB                          ARMOR FS                    Cloudflare → B2\n  │                                │                              │\n  ├─ read footer (last 8 bytes) ──▶├─ Range GET (encrypted) ────▶│\n  │◀── decrypted footer ──────────┤◀── ciphertext ──────────────┤\n  │                                │                              │\n  ├─ read col_a, row group 3 ────▶├─ Range GET (3 blocks) ─────▶│\n  │◀── decrypted column chunk ────┤◀── ciphertext ──────────────┤\n  │                                │                              │\n  └─ result set                    └                              └\n```\n\n---\n\n## 🔐 Security Model\n\n| 🛡️ Threat | ✅ Mitigation |\n|-----------|-------------|\n| B2 breach | Client-side encryption — B2 only stores opaque blobs |\n| Cloudflare inspection | Client-side encryption — Cloudflare only caches opaque blobs |\n| Man-in-the-middle | TLS everywhere + client-side encryption |\n| Key compromise | Envelope encryption — per-file DEKs limit blast radius; key rotation re-wraps without re-uploading |\n| Data corruption | SHA-256 integrity hash + per-block HMACs |\n| Unauthorized access | Private bucket + Cloudflare Worker auth + scoped application keys |\n\n---\n\n## 🔑 Encryption Design\n\n```\n🔑 Master Key (MEK)\n │  stored locally, never uploaded\n │\n └─▶ wraps ──▶ 🔑 Data Encryption Key (DEK)\n                │  random per-file, wrapped copy in B2 metadata\n                │\n                └─▶ encrypts ──▶ 📦 File Data\n                                   AES-256-CTR, 64KB blocks\n                                   per-block HMAC-SHA256\n                                   seekable random access\n```\n\n---\n\n## 🚀 Quick Start\n\n### Docker\n\n```bash\ndocker run -d \\\n  -p 9000:9000 \\\n  -p 9001:9001 \\\n  -e ARMOR_B2_REGION=us-east-005 \\\n  -e ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID=your-key-id \\\n  -e ARMOR_B2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=your-key-secret \\\n  -e ARMOR_BUCKET=your-bucket \\\n  -e ARMOR_CF_DOMAIN=your-cf-domain.example.com \\\n  -e ARMOR_MEK=$(openssl rand -hex 32) \\\n  -e ARMOR_AUTH_ACCESS_KEY=my-access-key \\\n  -e ARMOR_AUTH_SECRET_KEY=my-secret-key \\\n  ghcr.io/jedarden/armor:latest\n```\n\n### Client Configuration\n\nPoint any S3-compatible tool at ARMOR:\n\n```bash\n# AWS CLI\naws --endpoint-url http://localhost:9000 s3 cp file.txt s3://bucket/key\n\n# boto3 (Python)\nimport boto3\ns3 = boto3.client('s3',\n    endpoint_url='http://localhost:9000',\n    aws_access_key_id='my-access-key',\n    aws_secret_access_key='my-secret-key')\ns3.upload_file('local.txt', 'bucket', 'key')\n\n# DuckDB\nINSTALL httpfs;\nLOAD httpfs;\nSET s3_endpoint='localhost:9000';\nSET s3_access_key_id='my-access-key';\nSET s3_secret_access_key='my-secret-key';\nSELECT * FROM read_parquet('s3://bucket/data.parquet');\n```\n\n---\n\n## ⚙️ Configuration\n\nARMOR is configured via environment variables:\n\n| Variable | Required | Default | Description |\n|----------|----------|---------|-------------|\n| `ARMOR_LISTEN` | No | `0.0.0.0:9000` | S3 API listen address |\n| `ARMOR_ADMIN_LISTEN` | No | `127.0.0.1:9001` | Admin API (key rotation, canary, audit) |\n| `ARMOR_B2_REGION` | Yes | — | B2 region (e.g., `us-east-005`) |\n| `ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID` | Yes | — | B2 application key ID |\n| `ARMOR_B2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` | Yes | — | B2 application key |\n| `ARMOR_BUCKET` | Yes | — | B2 bucket name |\n| `ARMOR_CF_DOMAIN` | Yes | — | Cloudflare domain CNAME'd to B2 |\n| `ARMOR_MEK` | Yes | — | Master encryption key (hex, 32 bytes) |\n| `ARMOR_AUTH_ACCESS_KEY` | No | (random) | Client access key |\n| `ARMOR_AUTH_SECRET_KEY` | No | (random) | Client secret key |\n| `ARMOR_BLOCK_SIZE` | No | `65536` | Encryption block size |\n| `ARMOR_WRITER_ID` | No | (hostname) | Provenance chain writer ID |\n| `ARMOR_READYZ_CACHE_TTL` | No | `30` | Seconds to cache backend connectivity check in `/readyz` (only used when canary is disabled) |\n| `ARMOR_DASHBOARD_USER` | No | (empty) | Dashboard HTTP Basic Auth username |\n| `ARMOR_DASHBOARD_PASS` | No | (empty) | Dashboard HTTP Basic Auth password |\n| `ARMOR_DASHBOARD_TOKEN` | No | (empty) | Dashboard Bearer token for authentication |\n\n### Multi-Key Configuration\n\n```bash\n# Default key\nARMOR_MEK=<hex>\n\n# Named keys for different prefixes\nARMOR_MEK_SENSITIVE=<hex>\nARMOR_MEK_ARCHIVE=<hex>\n\n# Route prefixes to keys\nARMOR_KEY_ROUTES=\"data/pii/*=sensitive,archive/*=archive,*=default\"\n```\n\n### Multi-Credential Configuration\n\n```bash\n# Multiple auth credentials with ACLs\nARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_ACCESS_KEY=reader-key\nARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_SECRET_KEY=reader-secret\nARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_ACL=\"mybucket:readonly/*\"\n\nARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_ACCESS_KEY=writer-key\nARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_SECRET_KEY=writer-secret\nARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_ACL=\"mybucket:*,otherbucket:uploads/*\"\n```\n\n---\n\n## 📂 Repository Structure\n\n```\nARMOR/\n├── README.md\n├── Dockerfile\n├── go.mod\n├── go.sum\n├── cmd/armor/main.go          # Entrypoint\n├── internal/\n│   ├── server/                # S3 server, ├── presign/               # Pre-signed URL sharing\n│   ├── provenance/            # Cryptographic audit chain\n│   ├── logging/               # Structured JSON logging\n│   └── metrics/               # Prometheus metrics\n├── deploy/kubernetes/         # Kubernetes manifests\n├── tests/integration/         # Integration tests\n└── docs/\n    ├── dashboard.md           # Web dashboard documentation\n    ├── plan/                  # Implementation plan\n    └── research/              # Technical research\n```\n\n---\n\n## 🎨 Web Dashboard\n\nARMOR includes a web dashboard for bucket browsing, encryption status visualization, and cache statistics. The dashboard is accessible on the admin port (default `127.0.0.1:9001`):\n\n```bash\n# Local access\nopen http://localhost:9001/dashboard\n\n# Kubernetes port-forward\nkubectl port-forward svc/armor 9001:9001\nopen http://localhost:9001/dashboard\n```\n\n**Features:**\n- 📂 **Bucket browsing** - Navigate encrypted buckets with prefix-based navigation\n- 🔐 **Encryption status** - Visual badges showing which objects use ARMOR encryption and which keys\n- 📊 **Cache statistics** - Monitor metadata cache hit rates and effectiveness\n- 📈 **System metrics** - Real-time metrics for requests, bytes transferred, uptime, and canary status\n- 🔍 **Object details** - Detailed metadata view for individual objects\n\nFor full documentation, see [docs/dashboard.md](docs/dashboard.md).\n\n## 🔧 Admin API\n\nKey management endpoints on the admin listener (default `127.0.0.1:9001`):\n\n| Endpoint | Method | Description |\n|----------|--------|-------------|\n| `/healthz` | GET | Health check `/admin/key/rotate` | POST | Rotate MEK (re-wrap all DEKs) |\n| `/admin/key/export` | GET | Export current MEK (`?confirm=yes`) |\n| `/admin/audit` | GET | Walk provenance chains, verify integrity |\n| `/admin/presign` | POST | Generate pre-signed share URL |\n| `/armor/canary` | GET | Canary integrity status |\n| `/dashboard` | GET | Web dashboard UI |\n| `/dashboard/object` | GET | Object details (JSON) |\n| `/dashboard/metrics` | GET | Dashboard metrics (JSON) |\n\n---\n\n## 📋 S3 API Coverage\n\n### Transforming Operations (encryption/decryption)\n\n| Operation | Support {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_b91aba0733d6418d92454a21","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# ARMOR Implementation Plan\n2\t\n3\t> **Status: Implementation Complete** (as of 2026-03-24)\n4\t>\n5\t> All planned features from Phases 1-3 are implemented. The only remaining item is the optional web dashboard.\n6\t\n7\t## Overview\n8\t\n9\tARMOR is an S3-compatible proxy server that transparently encrypts and decrypts data between clients and Backblaze B2. Clients interact with ARMOR using standard S3 SDKs and tools (boto3, DuckDB `httpfs`, AWS CLI, etc.). ARMOR handles all cryptography invisibly — clients never see ciphertext.\n10\t\n11\t```\n12\t                        ARMOR Server\n13\t                    ┌───────────────────┐\n14\t                    │                   │\n15\t   S3 Protocol      │   ┌───────────┐   │     S3 Protocol\n16\t  (plaintext)       │   │ Encryption │   │    (ciphertext)\n17\t ──────────────────▶│   │   Layer    │───│──────────────────▶  Backblaze B2\n18\t Client / DuckDB    │   └───────────┘   │                     (storage)\n19\t ◀──────────────────│   ┌───────────┐   │◀──────────────────\n20\t  S3 Protocol       │   │ Decryption │   │    via Cloudflare\n21\t  (plaintext)       │   │   Layer    │   │    (free egress)\n22\t                    │   └───────────┘   │\n23\t                    │                   │\n24\t                    └───────────────────┘\n25\t```\n26\t\n27\t**What makes this different from encrypting on the client:** The encryption boundary is inside the server, not on each client machine. Any S3-compatible tool works unmodified — DuckDB, pandas, rclone, AWS CLI, custom scripts — they all point at `localhost:9000` (or wherever ARMOR listens) and get transparent encryption with zero-egress downloads through Cloudflare.\n28\t\n29\t### Statelessness Principle\n30\t\n31\tARMOR is **stateless by design.** Any ARMOR instance with the same configuration (MEK + B2 credentials + Cloudflare domain) can read, write, and manage the same data. There is no local state that is required for correctness:\n32\t\n33\t- **All authoritative state lives in B2.** Encryption metadata (IV, wrapped DEK, plaintext size) is stored in B2 object headers and the envelope prepended to each object. Operational metadata (key rotation progress, provenance chain) is stored as objects under a `.armor/` prefix in B2.\n34\t- **In-memory caches are optional performance optimizations**, not state.
